title: Lemon Rice
description: A classic South Indian rice dish flavored with fresh lemon juice, curry leaves, and a fragrant tempering of spices. Perfect as a light main or a side, often paired with yogurt, pickles, or papad.

ingredients.title: For the Rice
items: 1 cup (200 g) raw basmati or sona masoori rice

ingredients.title: For the Tempering (Tadka)
items: 2 tbsp sesame oil (or sunflower/groundnut oil for authenticity)
½ tsp cumin seeds (optional, South Indian homes often skip)
1–2 dried red chilies, broken in half

ingredients.title: For the Flavor Base
items: Juice of 1 large lemon (about 2 tbsp), freshly squeezed

instruction.title: Step 1: Cook the Rice
steps: Rinse rice in cold water 2–3 times until water runs clear.
In a pot, add rice, 2 cups water, salt, and oil/ghee.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook 10–12 minutes until water is absorbed.
Turn off heat and let sit (covered) for 5 minutes. Fluff gently with a fork.
Tip: Spread rice on a wide plate to cool slightly; this prevents clumping when mixing.

instruction.title: Step 2: Prepare the Tempering
steps: Heat sesame oil in a heavy pan or wok over medium heat.
Add mustard seeds; let them crackle.
Add cumin seeds (if using), dried red chilies, and green chilies. Sauté 20–30 seconds.
Add curry leaves (stand back, they splutter!).
Sprinkle in turmeric and asafoetida; stir quickly to avoid burning.

instruction.title: Step 3: Combine & Finish
steps: Add cooked rice to the pan with the tempering.
Season with additional salt if needed.
Drizzle in lemon juice and sugar; toss gently until evenly coated and golden yellow.
Turn off heat; let rest 2–3 minutes for flavors to meld.

servingSuggestions.title: Serving Suggestions
items: Serve warm or at room temperature in a wide, shallow bowl.
Garnish with extra curry leaves or a sprinkle of chopped coriander (optional).
Pair with yogurt, pickles, or papad.

chefTips: Rice texture: Slightly firm grains (not mushy) are key.
Oil choice: Sesame oil gives authentic aroma; ghee gives richness.
Lemon juice: Always add after turning off the heat to preserve fresh citrus flavor.
Make-ahead: This travels well—ideal for lunch boxes or picnics.
